sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 9.1 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.9)

statement.latex: A group$G$is said to be potent\/ if, to each$x G$and each positive integer$n$dividing the order of$x$(we suppose$$is divisible by every positive integer), there exists a finite homomorphic image of$G$in which the image of$x$has order precisely~$n$. Is the free product of two potent groups again potent?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
